label: AWS
description: Connect to Amazon Web Services to interact with S3, EC2, Lambda, and other AWS services using IAM credentials.

form.title: Connect AWS
form.subtitle: Authenticate with your IAM access keys or STS-issued temporary credentials to access AWS services.

methods.label: IAM Access Key
methods.description: Paste your IAM Access Key ID and Secret Access Key. The simplest and most common way to connect AWS programmatically — generate a key pair in the IAM console and paste it here.
methods.how_to:
## IAM Access Key

Generate a dedicated IAM user with only the permissions Anton needs, then paste the credentials below.

### Steps

1. Sign in to the [AWS Management Console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/) and open **IAM**.
2. Go to **Users → Create user**. Give it a name like `anton-integration`.
3. On the **Set permissions** step, attach only the policies you need (e.g. `AmazonS3ReadOnlyAccess`, `AWSLambda_ReadOnlyAccess`).
4. After the user is created, open the user → **Security credentials** tab → **Create access key**.
5. Select **Application running outside AWS** as the use case and click through.
6. Copy the **Access key ID** and **Secret access key** (the secret is shown only once — save it now).
7. Paste both values below, and set **Default region** to the AWS region your resources live in (e.g. `us-east-1`).

### Notes

- Follow the principle of least privilege — avoid using root account credentials or over-permissioned keys.
- If the key is ever exposed, deactivate it immediately in IAM → Security credentials.
- See [AWS best practices for access keys](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html#lock-away-credentials).

fields.name: access_key_id
fields.label: Access Key ID
fields.description: Starts with AKIA for long-term IAM credentials. Found in IAM → Users → Security credentials.

fields.name: secret_access_key
fields.label: Secret Access Key
fields.description: Paired with the Access Key ID. Shown only once at creation time — if lost, generate a new key pair.

fields.name: default_region
fields.label: Default Region
fields.description: The AWS region your primary resources are located in. You can override this per-request.

methods.label: STS Temporary Credentials
methods.description: Use short-lived credentials issued by AWS Security Token Service (STS) — typically from AssumeRole, federated login, or an EC2/Lambda instance profile. Requires an Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, and a session token.
methods.how_to:
## STS Temporary Credentials

STS credentials are short-lived tokens issued when you assume an IAM role, use federated identity, or retrieve credentials from an EC2/ECS/Lambda instance metadata service. They expire automatically.

### When to use this

- Your organization uses AWS SSO / IAM Identity Center and hands out temporary tokens.
- You're assuming a cross-account role via `aws sts assume-role`.
- You retrieved credentials from the EC2 instance metadata endpoint and want to paste them directly.

### Steps

1. Obtain temporary credentials from STS. For example, using the AWS CLI:
   - `aws sts assume-role --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/MyRole --role-session-name anton`
2. The response includes `AccessKeyId`, `SecretAccessKey`, and `SessionToken`.
3. Paste all three values below, along with the **Default region**.

### Notes

- STS credentials expire — the default session duration is 1 hour (up to 12 hours depending on role settings). Anton will return auth errors once they expire; you'll need to paste fresh credentials.
- For long-running access, prefer long-term IAM access keys (the **IAM Access Key** method) or configure automatic role assumption at the infrastructure level.
- See [AWS STS docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/STS/latest/APIReference/welcome.html) for full details.

fields.name: access_key_id
fields.label: Access Key ID
fields.description: Temporary access key IDs start with ASIA instead of AKIA.

fields.name: secret_access_key
fields.label: Secret Access Key
fields.description: The secret paired with the temporary access key ID.

fields.name: session_token
fields.label: Session Token
fields.description: The STS session token returned alongside the temporary access key. Required for all STS-issued credentials.

fields.name: default_region
fields.label: Default Region
fields.description: The AWS region your primary resources are located in.
